page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 360 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of 16 local NVMe SSDs that deliver a total capacity of 6167 GB. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz and includes a 504 MB L3 cache alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for memory bandwidth, recording read-only speeds of 7717.61 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 356.38 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Computational benchmarks indicate strong multi-core performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 110513.48 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 20352.0. The hardware-accelerated instruction sets contribute to top-tier performance in cryptography, ray tracing, and software compilation. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 171.76 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high compute and local storage capacity, though it lacks GPU acceleration, which limits its efficiency for heavy deep learning training. However, the inclusion of Intel AMX allows for capable CPU-based LLM inference, achieving 319.13 tokens per second for medium model prompt processing, though text generation speeds remain average. The high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong memory bandwidth make this configuration highly cost-efficient for data-intensive, low-latency workloads. It is optimally suited for high-performance database hosting, large-scale software compilation, real-time data processing, and web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a high-performance, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor, 360 GB of RAM, and 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.3 GHz with a 504 MB L3 cache, the processor supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmark data shows top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7717.61 GB/sec and exceptional database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 171.76 million operations per second. While the lack of dedicated GPUs limits heavy deep learning training, the integrated AMX instructions enable strong CPU-based LLM prompt processing. This configuration is highly efficient for low-latency databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd features 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a 96-vCPU server with 360 GB RAM and 6167 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-performance databases.
tagline: A high-core compute instance featuring massive local NVMe storage, optimized for low-latency database operations and high-throughput data processing.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz
6167 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives
360 GB of system memory with top-tier read bandwidth up to 7717.61 GB/sec
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Best suited for high-performance databases, software compilation, and web serving
